page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1.4xlarge is a storage-focused virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core.

A defining characteristic of this instance is its massive local storage capacity, which includes 47,244 GB of local HDD storage. This high density of local hard disk drive capacity is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are included in this configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.d1.4xlarge offers a high volume of local storage capacity per resource unit, making it a cost-effective option for workloads requiring massive sequential data storage without the premium cost of solid-state drives. The hardware profile is optimized for large-scale data warehousing, distributed file systems like HDFS, log processing, and cold storage applications where capacity is prioritized over high random IOPS.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1.4xlarge is a storage-optimized virtual server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, it provides 64.0 GB of RAM alongside a massive local storage capacity of 47,244 GB on HDD. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps and does not include GPU acceleration. This configuration offers high storage density per resource unit, making it highly cost-efficient for capacity-driven workloads. It is designed for distributed file systems, large-scale data warehousing, and cold data storage where massive sequential storage capacity is required.
